intro: Didiscus celebicus, also known as Celebes didiscus, is a plant species native to Indonesia, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide health benefits such as pain relief and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Didiscus celebicus include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
